_comment: ATLAS — atmosphere, sun, planets, moons, MCP star. Atlas-private. Sibling of planets.json (vendored). Per vision §8. Card copy passed through forbidden-words list + 4 voice tests + Sush voice fingerprints + ≤100 word budget. Plain AI + Curriculum carry the 🌱 free forever badge at TOP per constitutional firewall. Last voice pass: 8 May 2026.
_freshnessNote: Bump lastShippedAt (ISO date) when something visible ships on a planet. Within 14 days → subtle freshness pulse on its body. Manual today; sitemap.xml automation is V3 backlog.
_relationshipsNote: connectsTo[] surfaces cross-planet relationships when a body is focused. Use sparingly — Earth as hub; Agentic ↔ Claw as the technical pair; MCP rays already show its endpoint connections.

atmosphere.name: Deep Cosmos
atmosphere.monoFont: JetBrains Mono, ui-monospace, SFMono-Regular, Menlo, monospace

sun.label: the source
sun.tooltip: The cosmos is held together here. Every planet exists because something is feeding it.

planets.audience: Anyone learning Microsoft cloud or AI for the first time, especially from a non-tech background.
planets.content: Tools, mind maps, certification paths, and a long-running blog. Each one started because Sush needed it himself.
planets.founder: In my head, I'm still the curious intern. What overflows lands here.

moons.name: Guided
moons.audience: Anyone studying for a Microsoft cert who wants real practice without paying $79.
moons.content: Practice exams for AZ-900, AZ-104, AI-900, MS-900 and more. $9 per cert. One year of access.
moons.founder: Most cert-prep sites charge $79 for what should cost $9. Honest take? It still costs me to run.

planets.audience: Non-tech folks tired of not knowing what 'GCC High' actually means.
planets.content: A growing index of Microsoft jargon. Type `$_` to launch. One entry, one plain-English answer.
planets.founder: I forgot what GCC High meant for the third time. So I built CMD.

planets.audience: Anyone watching their role shift under AI and asking 'am I still safe in five years?'
planets.content: Honest news on jobs that grew, jobs that shrank, and jobs no one saw coming. New issue every Sunday.
planets.founder: I started reading job postings every weekend because the pace scared me. Now you can read them with me.

planets.badge: 🌱 free forever · no money angle, ever
planets.audience: Anyone confused by AI hype, doom, or jargon. Mums, partners, friends, you.
planets.content: Topics, weekly halts of what changed, and short paths through ideas. Plain words only. No hype, no doom.
planets.founder: If I can't explain it to my mum at the dinner table, I haven't understood it yet.

moons.name: Plain AI Curriculum
moons.badge: 🌱 free forever · no money angle, ever
moons.audience: Anyone wanting to navigate the AI wave one thing at a time. Starting with families.
moons.content: Short lessons in plain English. CC BY-SA 4.0. No login, no email capture, no money angle, ever.
moons.founder: Voluntary work for the global commons. No profit, no glory. Pure value.

planets.audience: Developers and SREs building with agents. Cockpit-density UI. Mostly dark.
planets.content: A field-guide for what to build, where to plug in, what to ship. Cyan, green, amber signal palette.
planets.founder: If you're already in MCP, function-calls, and orchestration land — this is where you sit.

planets.audience: Anyone trying to understand how OpenClaw actually works without reading 200-page specs.
planets.content: A reading reference with sidebar TOC, margin notes, and a long calm column. Light or dark.
planets.founder: I had to read OpenClaw three times. So I wrote down the version I wish I'd had.

mcp.name: MCP Move
mcp.audience: Claude, ChatGPT, Copilot, your agents, your scripts. Anything that speaks MCP.
mcp.content: An MCP move that exposes the cosmos. Per-planet endpoints under construction. CMD live first.
mcp.founder: If a future agent wants to recommend the right cert or jargon entry, here's the API.

_externalNote: External channels — not planets, not destinations inside the cosmos. They sit OUTSIDE the planet orbits and connect the cosmos to the wider world. Two YouTube broadcasts (long-form, bites) and Ko-fi (give + receive). Two render styles available via ?ext= URL param: 'comets' (default — orbital with tails for YouTubes, fixed beacon for Ko-fi) and 'outposts' (all three on a fixed outer ring). Render mode is a SUSH visual choice — voice + content stay the same.

channels.name: Long-form
channels.audience: Anyone who learns better when someone walks them through it on screen.
channels.content: Full-length tutorials and walk-throughs. Microsoft cloud, AI, the curious-intern lens.
channels.founder: I make these because writing alone doesn't always carry it. Sometimes you need to see my screen.

channels.name: Bites
channels.audience: Anyone who wants a 60-second answer instead of a 15-minute video.
channels.content: Bite-sized cuts of the long-form videos. Just the answer, no setup.
channels.founder: Same content, less time. For when you just want the bit you came for.

channels.name: Ko-fi
channels.audience: Anyone who wants the free assets, or wants to grab a paid one for ≤$5, or wants to drop a coffee.
channels.content: Free downloadable assets. Paid ones capped at $5 to keep them accessible. Tip jar if something here helped.
channels.founder: If something here gave you something, this is where you can give a little back. No pressure — most things are free, the rest are capped at $5.
